case_title: NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC. VS. MARGARITO PAZ ET AL

SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100066322), PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC. SERVED MAR-03-2019, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON NATURAL PERSON, MAILING DATE MAR-08-2019 AS TO DEFENDANT PAZ, MARGARITO AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND DBA PAZ DEMO
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
DECLARATION RE: INTEREST (TRANSACTION ID # 100070243)
DECLARATION CCP 585 (TRANSACTION ID # 100070243) FILED BY PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC.

UNDER APPLICATION TO THE CLERK FOR JUDGMENT UNDER SEC 585(1) C.C.P., IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC. RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT PAZ, MARGARITO AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND DBA PAZ DEMO $36,547.82 PRINCIPAL, $7,897.89 INTEREST, $489.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $44,934.71, SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100070243)
OPPOSITION TO JUDGMENT DEBTOR MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100080634) FILED BY PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARGARITO PAZ'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED. TO OBTAIN RELIEF UNDER CODE OF CIV. PROC., 473.5, THE MOVING PARTY MUST SHOW THAT "SERVICE OF SUMMONS HAS NOT RESULTED IN ACTUAL NOTICE . . . IN TIME TO DEFEND THE ACTION." DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION SUFFICIENTLY MEETS THAT BURDEN, SETTING FORTH THAT HE DID NOT HAVE ACTUAL NOTICE BECAUSE HE NO LONGER LIVED AT THE ADDRESS AT WHICH PLAINTIFF ATTEMPTED SUBSTITUTED SERVICE, THE MCLAUGHLIN RESIDENCE, AND BECAUSE OF HIS ESTRANGED RELATIONSHIP WITH HIS SISTER, WHO DID NOT NOTIFY HIM THAT SHE HAD RECEIVED THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT. (PAZ DECL. 3-4, 6, 17 ["I HAD NO IDEA THAT THEY HAD SUED ME."]; SECOND PAZ DECL. 2, 4, 5, 15; SEE ELLARD V. CONWAY (2001) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 540, 547 ["'[A]CTUAL NOTICE' IN SECTION 473.5 'MEANS GENUINE KNOWLEDGE OF THE PARTY LITIGANT.' [CITATION.] 'ACTUAL KNOWLEDGE' HAS BEEN STRICTLY CONSTRUED, WITH THE AIM OF IMPLEMENTING THE POLICY OF LIBERALLY GRANTING RELIEF SO THAT CASES MAY BE RESOLVED ON THEIR MERITS."].) DEFENDANT SHALL FILE AN ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT WITHIN 30 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THE COURT'S ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO, CSR#8600, E-MAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ALEXANDER J. PERZ, ESQ. of Law Office of Michael Heath (Tel: 415-931-4207) appearing for defendant Margarito Paz;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MARGARITO PAZ'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Margarito Paz's motion to set aside default is granted. To obtain relief under Code of Civ. Proc., 473.5, the moving party must show that "service of summons has not resulted in actual notice . . . in time to defend the action." Defendant's declaration sufficiently meets that burden, setting forth that he did not have actual notice because he no longer lived at the address at which plaintiff attempted substituted service, the McLaughlin residence, and because of his estranged relationship with his sister, who did not notify him that she had received the summons and complaint. (Paz Decl. 3-4, 6, 17 ["I had no idea that they had sued me."]; Second Paz Decl. 2, 4, 5, 15; see Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 547 ["'[A]ctual notice' in section 473.5 'means genuine knowledge of the party litigant.' [Citation.] 'Actual knowledge' has been strictly construed, with the aim of implementing the policy of liberally granting relief so that cases may be resolved on their merits."].) Defendant shall file an answer to the complaint within 30 days of entry of the court's order.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-21-2019 Off Calendar
